summary: The `pnpm audit` command detected a moderate-severity vulnerability in the `ip-address` package (XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods, versions <=10.1.0), causing the audit step to exit with code 1 and fail the CI job.
root_cause: A known security vulnerability (GHSA-v2v4-37r5-5v8g) exists in the transitive dependency `ip-address` (<=10.1.0) reachable via `network__fetch>@pnpm/network.agent>@pnpm/network.proxy-agent>socks-proxy-agent>socks>ip-address`. The `pnpm audit` command found 1 moderate vulnerability and exited with code 1, failing the Audit dependencies job.

evidence.quote: │ moderate            │ ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods   │
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific vulnerability detected by pnpm audit that caused the failure.
Shows the affected version range of the ip-address package currently in use.
Shows the dependency chain through which the vulnerable ip-address package is pulled in.
Confirms pnpm audit found a vulnerability, which caused it to exit non-zero.
Confirms the audit command failed with exit code 1, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Update the `ip-address` transitive dependency to >=10.1.1 (the patched version) by upgrading the parent packages in the dependency chain (`socks`, `socks-proxy-agent`, `@pnpm/network.proxy-agent`, or `@pnpm/network.agent`) so that `ip-address` resolves to a version >=10.1.1.
